title: Full-Height Playhouse Loft Bed
summary: A loft bed enclosed as a playhouse — plywood walls with trimmed windows and a door, a decorative vent, and a built-in dowel ladder. An ambitious, rewarding build.
description:
This is one of the most involved projects in the catalog: a full-height loft bed whose sides are closed in with plywood and dressed as a little house, complete with trimmed windows that open on hinges, a front door, a gable vent, and an integrated dowel ladder. It's a lot of parts and a lot of fitting, and the source plan deliberately gives cut lengths step-by-step rather than as one list, because so much is cut-to-fit — so treat the cut list below as the structural skeleton and measure the trim off the real assembly as you go.

The structure is a 2x4 platform frame skinned in 1/2" plywood, stood up on 2x4 legs, with 3/4" plywood house panels trimmed in 1x2s and 1x4s. Get the platform frame perfectly square before skinning it — every panel and every window that follows references off it, and a racked frame throws all of them out. The false-front trim measurements in the plan are explicitly approximations; take exact measurements on your actual build.

Two child-safety notes carry real weight here. Leave generous gaps around the hinged window and door panels so little fingers can't get pinched. And the finished bed must be anchored: screw the assembled bed into wall studs with screws at least 3-1/2" long on the open side — a full-height loft bed that isn't fixed to the wall is a tip-over hazard. This is a build to take slowly, dry-fitting as you go.

tools.note: Breaking down four-plus sheets of plywood.

materials.name: 1x2 board, 8 ft
materials.note: Window and panel trim — a bundle.

cutList.note: Additional house panels cut to fit per the step-by-step plan.

steps.title: Build the platform frame
steps.body: Build the bed frame from two 2x4 sides at 75" and six 2x4 studs at 36", screwed with 3" screws. Make it perfectly square — every panel and window downstream references off this frame.

steps.title: Skin the platform
steps.body: Cut 1/2" plywood at 75" x 39" and attach it to the frame with 2" screws and glue, outside edges flush. Confirm the frame is perfectly square before fastening the plywood — the sheet locks in whatever shape the frame is holding.

steps.title: Stand up the house frame
steps.body: Screw the 51-1/2" 2x4 corner uprights to the bed frame (bottoms flush with the plywood), then add the 48" 2x4 and 2x6 top rails, 2x4 first with bottom edges flush, then the 2x6 keeping the outside corner flush. This is a three-sided house; the open side gets screwed to the wall studs.

steps.title: Panel and trim the walls
steps.body: Skin the sides with 3/4" plywood and trim out the false front with 1x2s and 1x4s. The plan's dimensions here are approximations — take exact measurements off your build. Trim windows with 1x2s (sides first, then top and bottom), and add the gable vent, whose vertical pieces also help hold the two plywood sheets together.

steps.title: Hang windows and doors with pinch gaps
steps.body: Cut the window doors from 1x6s and the front door from 1x12s to your openings, and hang them on hinges — leaving a generous gap around each so little fingers can't get pinched. This gap is a safety requirement, not a cosmetic choice.

steps.title: Build the ladder, finish, and anchor
steps.body: Build the ladder by boring 1-1/2" holes in the 2x4 ladder rails and gluing the dowels in. Finish the entire bed as desired. Then assemble: screw the legs on securely and screw the bed into wall studs on the open side with screws at least 3-1/2" long. A full-height loft bed must be anchored to the wall — do not skip it.

images.alt: Full-height loft bed enclosed as a playhouse with windows and a door
